title: --rules flag missing from validate and export commands
body:
## Problem

The `--rules` flag is only available on the `generate` command. Users who want to verify that a rules file produces constraint-valid data must run the full `generate` pipeline (writing output files) even when they only want validation feedback. Similarly, the `export` command has no way to apply column-level rules before exporting.

## Current Behaviour

```
# Only generate accepts --rules today
dotnet run --project ./Concoction.Cli -- generate --rules ./rules.yaml ...

# validate and export ignore rules entirely
dotnet run --project ./Concoction.Cli -- validate ...
dotnet run --project ./Concoction.Cli -- export --format json ...
```

## Expected Behaviour

```
# validate: apply rules, check constraints, print summary, exit 3 if issues
dotnet run --project ./Concoction.Cli -- validate \
  --provider sqlite \
  --connection "Data Source=./mydb.db" \
  --rules ./rules.yaml \
  --rows 50

# export: apply rules before exporting
dotnet run --project ./Concoction.Cli -- export \
  --provider sqlite \
  --connection "Data Source=./mydb.db" \
  --rules ./rules.yaml \
  --format json
```

## Proposed Change

1. Add `--rules` option to the `validate` command in `Concoction.Cli/Program.cs`.
2. Load, validate, and merge the rules file using `IRuleConfigurationService` (same logic already in `generate`).
3. Pass the merged `RuleConfiguration` to `ISyntheticDataOrchestrator.GenerateAsync`.
4. Add `--rules` option to the `export` command with the same loading logic.
5. Extract the shared rule-loading logic into a helper method to avoid duplication.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `validate` command accepts optional `--rules <path>` and applies rules before generating.
- [ ] `export` command accepts optional `--rules <path>` and applies rules before exporting.
- [ ] Rules validation errors on `validate` / `export` print to stderr and exit with code 1.
- [ ] Unit/integration tests: rules applied on validate produces correct output; invalid rules file exits 1.
- [ ] Documentation updated in `docs/how-to/cli-quickstart.md` and `docs/user-guide.md`.

title: Per-table row counts in the Rules DSL
body:
## Problem

The `--rows` CLI flag sets a single row count applied uniformly to every table. Real schemas typically need different volumes per table — more orders than customers, many line items per order, a small lookup table of currencies. There is currently no way to express this in the rules file.

## Proposed Change

Add an optional `rows` field to the `TableRule` model:

```yaml
version: "1"
tables:
  - table: "public.customers"
    rows: 100
    columns: []

  - table: "public.orders"
    rows: 500
    columns:
      - column: "status"
        distribution:
          pending: 0.4
          shipped: 0.6

  - table: "public.line_items"
    rows: 2000
    columns: []
```

When `rows` is specified in the rules file it takes precedence over `--rows`. Tables without a `rows` entry continue to use the global `--rows` value.

## Domain / Model Changes

- Add `int? Rows { get; init; }` to `TableRule` in `Concoction.Domain/Models/RuleConfiguration.cs`.
- Validate that `rows`, when present, is ≥ 1.
- In `Concoction.Cli/Program.cs`, after loading and merging rules, build `rowCounts` by merging the `--rows` default with per-table overrides from the rules file.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TableRule` has optional `Rows` property (`int?`).
- [ ] `IRuleConfigurationService.Validate()` rejects `rows < 1`.
- [ ] `generate` command uses per-table `rows` when present, falling back to `--rows`.
- [ ] `validate` and `export` commands honour the same per-table row counts (after #rules-dsl-validate-export-flag).
- [ ] `RuleConfigurationService.Merge()` preserves the higher-specificity `rows` value during merge.
- [ ] Unit tests: per-table override, global fallback, validation error for rows < 1.
- [ ] Documentation updated in `docs/how-to/rules-dsl.md` and `docs/user-guide.md`.

title: Table-level column defaults in the Rules DSL
body:
## Problem

Rules currently operate at the individual column level. There is no way to set a default `nullRate`, `strategy`, or `seedOffset` that applies to every column in a table unless explicitly overridden. For wide tables with many nullable columns this means repetitive per-column entries.

## Proposed Change

Add a `columnDefaults` object at the `TableRule` level:

```yaml
version: "1"
tables:
  - table: "public.events"
    columnDefaults:
      nullRate: 0.2        # 20 % null for every column unless overridden
    columns:
      - column: "event_type"
        nullRate: 0.0      # override: never null
        distribution:
          click: 0.5
          view: 0.3
          purchase: 0.2
      - column: "user_id"
        nullRate: 0.0      # override: never null
        strategy: "Integer"
```

`columnDefaults` is lower-precedence than any explicit `columns[]` entry for the same column. It participates in the existing precedence chain:

```
global defaults < project defaults < table rules < columnDefaults < column rules
```

## Domain / Model Changes

- Add `ColumnDefaults? ColumnDefaults { get; init; }` to `TableRule`.
- `ColumnDefaults` mirrors `ColumnRule` but omits `column` (not applicable) and `jsonPaths` (path-level defaults are out of scope).
- `RuleConfigurationService.Merge()` applies `columnDefaults` as a lower-priority fallback for columns in that table.
- `IRuleConfigurationService.Validate()` validates `ColumnDefaults` fields (e.g. `nullRate` in [0,1]).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `ColumnDefaults` record added to `Concoction.Domain/Models/RuleConfiguration.cs` with `Strategy?`, `NullRate?`, `SeedOffset?` fields.
- [ ] `TableRule.ColumnDefaults` property added.
- [ ] Validate: `nullRate` [0,1], `strategy` must be a recognised `DataKind` name.
- [ ] `ReferentialRowMaterializer` applies `columnDefaults` when no explicit column rule exists.
- [ ] Explicit column rule overrides any field set in `columnDefaults`.
- [ ] Unit tests: default nullRate applied; explicit column rule overrides default; default strategy applied; merge respects precedence.
- [ ] Documentation updated in `docs/how-to/rules-dsl.md` and `docs/user-guide.md`.
